figures.caption: Fig. 4. The GenerativeMPC hardware platform: differential-drive base with two 6-DoF SO101 arms (Feetech STS3215 servos), Velodyne VLP-16 LiDAR, and RealSense D435 RGB-D camera, running on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano under ROS 2 Humble.
Fig. 5. Velodyne VLP-16 point cloud captured during hardware experiments, showing the 3D scan of the environment used for obstacle detection in the MPC cost function.

Fig. 3. GenerativeMPC three-layer system architecture. Layer 1 (VLM-RAG) processes camera images and natural language instructions, outputting velocity limits and safety margin dsafe to the Whole-Body MPC, and stiffness Ki and damping Di gains to the impedance-admittance controller. Layer 2 (Whole-Body MPC) plans coordinated trajectories at 10 Hz over N = 7 steps with embedded APF obstacle avoidance. Layer 3 (Impedance-Admittance) executes compliant tracking at 50 Hz for both arms and mobile base.
Fig. 6. MuJoCo warehouse simulation environment with cylindrical obstacles and human-shaped mannequins used for controller development and human-aware navigation experiments.
